title: Prompt Engineering: Get More Out

modules.title: Why Your Prompts Are Probably Failing (and the Fix)
modules.sub: Diagnose the common mistakes that make AI responses useless
modules.description: You examine real examples of weak prompts and trace exactly why they produce vague, off-target, or unhelpful responses. By the end, you can spot the failure patterns in your own prompts and apply a straightforward fix that immediately improves results.

modules.title: The Core Techniques: Role, Context, Format, and Constraints
modules.sub: Master the four building blocks every strong prompt needs
modules.description: You learn to construct prompts using four key levers — assigning a role, supplying context, specifying a format, and setting constraints — and practice combining them. You leave with a repeatable structure you can apply to almost any request across any AI tool.

modules.title: Prompting for Your Goals: Writing, Coding, Research, and More
modules.sub: Tailor your prompting approach to specific real-world tasks
modules.description: You work through task-specific prompting strategies for common use cases like drafting content, generating code, and synthesizing research. You discover how the same core techniques shift depending on what you're actually trying to produce.

modules.title: Chaining Prompts: Getting Complex Work Done in Steps
modules.sub: Break big tasks into sequences that AI can actually handle
modules.description: You learn why a single prompt often can't carry a complex project and how to design a chain of connected prompts that builds toward a larger goal. You practice mapping out a multi-step workflow and passing outputs from one prompt into the next.

modules.title: Testing and Improving: How to Know When a Prompt Is Actually Good
modules.sub: Evaluate and iterate until your prompts reliably deliver
modules.description: You develop a simple testing mindset — running variations, comparing outputs, and identifying what changed and why. By the end you have a personal checklist for deciding when a prompt is good enough and when it still needs work.

modules.title: Prompting Across Tools: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Others
modules.sub: Adapt your skills to the quirks of each major AI platform
modules.description: You explore how different AI tools respond differently to the same prompt and what adjustments make each one perform at its best. You build a mental map of the key differences so you can switch between tools without starting from scratch.

modules.title: Building a Personal Prompt Library You'll Actually Use
modules.sub: Create a reusable collection of prompts built around your life
modules.description: You design and organize a personal prompt library tailored to your specific goals, workflows, and recurring tasks. You leave with a working library you can open immediately and keep expanding as your needs evolve.

modules.title: Advanced Patterns: Few-Shot, Chain-of-Thought, and When They Help
modules.sub: Unlock powerful techniques for harder, higher-stakes requests
modules.description: You learn when and how to use advanced prompting patterns — giving the AI examples to follow, or walking it through reasoning step by step — and practice applying them to genuinely difficult tasks. You finish with a clear sense of which pattern fits which situation so you're not overcomplicating simple jobs.

synopsis: The difference between AI giving you something usable and AI wasting your time is almost always the prompt â and it's a learnable skill, not a talent. This course gives you a practical system for writing prompts that get real results across every major AI tool you'll use.
rationale: Prompt engineering is the highest-leverage transferable skill for AI users across all tools and domains, yet most young adults are improvising rather than working from proven techniques.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can write structured, effective prompts for any major AI tool and build a personal library of reusable prompts tailored to your work and goals.
